must_detect.matched_text:
ESG data lake used for CSRD/SFDR metrics and investor reporting on the Aurelia Regulated Market; preliminary assessment indicates potential inside information within the meaning of EU MAR Articles 7 and 17.
Ms. Elina Markovic (born 1985-11-22), Sustainability Reporting Lead at Orphicora Energy SE in Zagreb, accessed the “GreenScope” workspace from corporate laptop OE-ZG-EM-022 during session S-7f3a at 19:42 CEST, using mailbox emarkovic@orphicora.eu and Teams profile “emarkovic_ZG”; subsequent anomalous API calls were executed under her role-based account.
Root cause analysis shows an OAuth token replay via a compromised third-party browser extension; device telemetry referenced Wi‑Fi “OE-Zagreb-3F” and a truncated client IP 10.44.XX.91, after which automated exports included Scope 2 energy invoices and facility shift rosters tied to the Zagreb and Varaždin sites.
Cross-border transfer context: the ESG dataset was routed to a non-EEA processor, Quayshore Analytics Inc. (fictional, Vesper City, US), under the 2021 EU SCCs and a transfer impact assessment; transfers were paused at 21:05 CEST pending supplementary measures and processor audit under Article 28 GDPR.
Special-category data (GDPR Art. 9): two weekly health-safety incident logs with anonymisation errors contained sick-leave annotations and union-related meeting attendance tags for maintenance staff; these files were included in the same export batch and are being remediated.
MAR workflow: the unvalidated variance in electricity consumption factors could lead to a restatement of Q2 emissions intensity, potentially affecting cost-of-capital assumptions in an unannounced green financing programme (project codename “Citrine”); the event is currently classified as inside information and delay of disclosure has been decided with documented legitimate interest and confidentiality controls.
Insider list entries were updated at 22:30 CEST (core team and external advisors), trading restrictions were reaffirmed to PDMRs, and the Aurelia Market Monitoring Unit was informed of a precautionary watch; no trading halt has been requested at this stage.
Data protection measures: data subjects are being identified, Article 33 GDPR notification to the Ardellia DPA has been prepared within 72 hours, and data minimisation plus pseudonymisation are being applied to any cross-border diagnostic artefacts.
No national ID numbers, passport details, full IBANs, or biometric templates were observed in the exfiltrated subset; supplier numbers appear internal-only (e.g., “VEND-4097-22”) and are being rotated.
Generic workforce demographics in the sustainability annex (average age 41, 62% technical roles, multiple EU languages) were referenced in dashboards but do not identify any person on their own.
Separate weak identifiers noted elsewhere but not linkable to a named person include: “a 29-year-old Rotterdam technician using an Android device on SSID ‘DeltaNet’ during night shift” in a chat snippet, and “a contractor in Plovdiv with badge series P-3A” in a warehouse ticket; no contact details or names attached.
A subtle signal surfaced in calendar metadata: recurring “EU Taxonomy mapping” meetings tagged “CEST-0800, Varaždin floor 2” with room beacon IDs; these have been purged from sharing links and replaced with generic descriptors.
For SFDR and CSRD purposes, we will issue a corrigendum if intensity metrics materially change; the current internal estimate is a 3–5% variance, but this is subject to assurance and may not be relied upon for investment decisions.
